result.markdown: --- title: "[rmst_test()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Test for equality of RMST across two or more groups. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_rmst.py#L280-L487){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python rmst_test( surv, tau, group, *, estimand="difference", strata=None, conf_level=0.95 ) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Compares restricted mean survival time (RMST) up to a fixed time tau across groups using a z-test or t-test. Provides point estimate, standard error, confidence interval, and p-value for the null hypothesis of equal RMST. For two groups, this is equivalent to a z-test on the RMST difference (default) or log-ratio (if estimand="ratio"). ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [surv]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Surv]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A right-censored `Surv` response (time-to-event data). [tau]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : The restriction time, typically a clinically relevant horizon (e.g., 365, 1825). [group]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Group membership for each observation. Can be array-like or categorical variable. [estimand]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["difference"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Type of estimand: `"difference"` (default, RMST1 - RMST2), `"ratio"` (RMST1 / RMST2), or `"percentage_difference"` ((RMST1 - RMST2) / RMST2 * 100). [strata]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : (Optional) Stratification variable for stratified RMST comparison. If provided, per-group RMST estimates are computed separately within each stratum and then combined using inverse-variance weights (Kaplan-Meier Greenwood variance). Strata in which either group is absent are skipped. [conf_level]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[0.95]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Confidence level for confidence intervals (the default is `0.95` for 95% CI). ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [RMSTResult]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A result object containing estimate, standard error, confidence interval, test statistic, and p-value. ::: ## Details {.doc-details} For two groups (i=1,2), the RMST difference is: $$ \Delta = \mathrm{RMST}_1(\tau) - \mathrm{RMST}_2(\tau) $$ with standard error: $$ \mathrm{SE}(\Delta) = \sqrt{\mathrm{SE}(\mathrm{RMST}_1)^2 + \mathrm{SE}(\mathrm{RMST}_2)^2} $$ assuming independence. The z-statistic is $Z = \Delta / \mathrm{SE}(\Delta)$, with two-tailed p-value from the standard normal. For ratio estimand, the log-ratio variance uses the delta method: $$ \mathrm{SE}(\log R) = \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{SE}_1^2}{\mathrm{RMST}_1^2} + \frac{\mathrm{SE}_2^2}{\mathrm{RMST}_2^2}} $$ When `strata` is provided, per-group RMST values within each stratum are combined using inverse-variance (Greenwood) weights: $$ \widehat{\mathrm{RMST}}_k = \frac{\sum_s w_{ks}\, \mathrm{RMST}_{ks}}{\sum_s w_{ks}}, \quad w_{ks} = \frac{1}{\mathrm{SE}_{ks}^2} $$ The stratified SE is $1 / \sqrt{\sum_s w_{ks}}$. The test statistic is then computed from the pooled group-level estimates. ## Examples {.doc-examples} Test whether the two sexes in the lung cancer dataset have significantly different one-year RMST: ::: {#9c11a323 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw # Load data and build a right-censored response lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) # Test for one-year RMST difference between sex groups result = gw.rmst_test(y, tau=365, group=lung["sex"]) result ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` RMSTResult(method='RMST difference (tau=365)', estimate=-55.9703, se=14.9581, 95% CI=[-85.2877, -26.6529], p_value=0.0001827) ``` ::: ::: Individual components of the result are accessible as attributes. For example, the point estimate and its p-value: ::: {#97a2cd0b .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Extract the RMST difference estimate result.estimate ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` -55.97032433997978 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#7fb69c70 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Extract the p-value for the hypothesis test result.p_value ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` 0.00018270646131113288 ``` ::: ::: To express the comparison as a ratio instead of a difference, set `estimand="ratio"`. This reports $\text{RMST}_1 / \text{RMST}_2$ with a log-scale confidence interval: ::: {#7deabd79 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Compare RMST as a ratio instead of a difference gw.rmst_test(y, tau=365, group=lung["sex"], estimand="ratio") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` RMSTResult(method='RMST ratio (tau=365)', estimate=0.8118, se=0.0456, 95% CI=[0.7272, 0.9063], p_value=0.0002068) ``` ::: :::
